crash_setup_explanation: Early crash setup signal: volatility stress is visible and credit-sensitive markets are weakening, but broad credit stress is not yet confirmed.
market_phase_explanation: Investors are becoming more defensive. Volatility is rising and risk assets are weakening, but systemic crisis conditions are not yet confirmed.
market_phase_drivers: volatility rising | credit-sensitive assets weakening | market breadth weakening | growth assets weakening | USD strength / tighter conditions
bond_regime_explanation: Government bonds may benefit from mild flight-to-safety demand while bond market volatility remains contained.

macro_cycle_drivers: global liquidity conditions | inflation trend | oil shock | dollar strength
macro_regime_explanation: Liquidity conditions are becoming less supportive and the stronger dollar suggests tighter global financial conditions.
structural_vulnerability_explanation: Structural vulnerability is elevated. The system may be more fragile if market stress increases.
system_risk_interpretation: Scenario C: Market stress is rising while structural vulnerability is already elevated. This is a more dangerous combination because stress is building on top of a weaker system.
